title: Air Metagenomics

supported_entry_points.label: Raw FAST5 or POD5 to end-to-end workflow
supported_entry_points.notes: Start with basecalling, then continue through the modular bash workflow.

supported_entry_points.label: Basecalled FASTQ to downstream metagenomics
supported_entry_points.notes: Enter at read-level QC and downstream metagenomics processing.

default_preprocessing.input_expectation: Raw FAST5 or POD5, or already basecalled FASTQ for air metagenomics.
default_preprocessing.basecalling: Use Guppy HAC for legacy FAST5 or Dorado HAC for POD5. The validated personal reference pipeline uses Dorado HAC v4.3.0 for POD5 inputs.
default_preprocessing.demultiplexing: Required only when barcoded multiplexed runs have not yet been separated by sample.
default_preprocessing.adapter_trimming: Trim adapters with Porechop after basecalling and before NanoFilt.
default_preprocessing.length_quality_filtering: NanoFilt with Q >= 8 and minimum length 100 bp.
default_preprocessing.additional_preprocessing: Phase-specific read subsampling with SeqKit before taxonomic comparison.

execution_model: Interactive wrapper and modular scripts in the group repo, plus a validated curated overview in Tim_Reska.
notes: Optimized for ultra-low biomass air samples collected by liquid impingement.
Best fit when the objective includes taxonomic profiling, assembly, binning, and functional interpretation from environmental DNA.
constraints: Not a dedicated soil metagenomics workflow.
Study-specific preprocessing differs between the greenhouse pilot and urban campaign.
decision_notes: Air Metagenomics is the nearest environmental DNA route for unsupported non-water metagenomic queries, but it should not be treated as a soil protocol.
This workflow is strongest when the sample is truly an air or bioaerosol collection.

title: Wetland Health

supported_entry_points.label: Basecalled FASTQ into unified wetland launcher
supported_entry_points.notes: The group workflow starts from basecalled FASTQ and then routes to the selected branch.

default_preprocessing.input_expectation: Raw input FASTQ directories for dual DNA/RNA wetland surveillance and related branches.
default_preprocessing.basecalling: The published workflow assumes basecalled reads. The curated personal reference uses Dorado SUP v5.0.0 for the main wetland pipeline.
default_preprocessing.demultiplexing: Required when raw data still contain pooled barcodes or multi-branch inputs that need per-sample separation.
default_preprocessing.adapter_trimming: Use Porechop where the branch still requires adapter removal before downstream analysis.
default_preprocessing.length_quality_filtering: Branch-specific thresholds apply; the track card below shows the relevant defaults.
default_preprocessing.additional_preprocessing: Choose the correct branch: DNA metagenomics, RNA virome, eDNA metabarcoding, or AIV WGS.

execution_model: Interactive wrapper plus track-specific workflows, with a curated personal overview in Tim_Reska for the full multi-omics study.
notes: Best fit for passive wetland water sampling with multiple analytical branches.
Includes DNA metagenomics, RNA viromics, vertebrate eDNA metabarcoding, and AIV whole-genome sequencing.
constraints: Not a generic environmental DNA workflow for arbitrary water or soil matrices.
The published group repo centers on basecalled FASTQ entry rather than raw POD5.
decision_notes: Wetland Health is an umbrella workflow and should usually resolve to a branch-specific track before execution.
Use this only when the study context is actually the passive wetland sampling system documented in the repository.

track_notes.title: Track 1: Shotgun Metagenomics (DNA)
track_notes.summary: Use the DNA branch when the goal is microbial community characterization, pathogen detection, AMR profiling, assembly, and plasmid screening from passive wetland water samples.

preprocessing_override.basecalling: Dorado SUP v5.0.0 in the curated personal reference pipeline.
preprocessing_override.demultiplexing: Demultiplex if pooled samples are not yet separated.
preprocessing_override.length_quality_filtering: NanoFilt with Q >= 9 and minimum length 100 bp.
preprocessing_override.additional_preprocessing: SeqKit subsampling to 87k reads for the metagenomics comparison branch.

notes: This is the nearest branch for passive-water pathogen and AMR surveillance.

track_notes.title: Track 2: RNA Virome
track_notes.summary: Use the RNA virome branch for untargeted viral community analysis from wetland RNA rather than targeted influenza reconstruction.

preprocessing_override.basecalling: Published workflow assumes basecalled FASTQ input for the RNA branch.
preprocessing_override.demultiplexing: Demultiplex if the RNA libraries are still pooled.
preprocessing_override.adapter_trimming: Use the published branch-specific read cleaning before assembly.
preprocessing_override.length_quality_filtering: NanoFilt with Q >= 9 and minimum length 100 bp.
preprocessing_override.additional_preprocessing: Proceed to nanoMDBG assembly, Medaka polishing, and DIAMOND BLASTx viral classification.

notes: Use this branch when you want viral-metagenome discovery rather than influenza-only consensus calling.

track_notes.title: Track 3: eDNA Metabarcoding
track_notes.summary: Use the eDNA branch for vertebrate host-range inference from wetland passive water samples using tagged amplicon data.

preprocessing_override.basecalling: The documented branch assumes basecalled amplicon reads rather than raw-signal basecalling inside this workflow.
preprocessing_override.demultiplexing: OBITools4 obimultiplex for barcoded 12S amplicon data.
preprocessing_override.length_quality_filtering: VSEARCH quality filtering with maxEE 1.0, singleton removal, and downstream OTU clustering.
preprocessing_override.additional_preprocessing: Global alignment against MIDORI2, then regional occurrence validation.

notes: Choose this branch only when the wetland study context applies. For broader water eDNA metabarcoding, Zambia eDNA may be the better fit.

track_notes.title: Track 4: AIV Whole-Genome Sequencing
track_notes.summary: Use the AIV branch when the wetland sample itself is the source of targeted avian-influenza genome reconstruction and segment-level interpretation.

preprocessing_override.basecalling: Published workflow assumes basecalled reads for the AIV branch.
preprocessing_override.demultiplexing: Demultiplex if barcode pooling remains unresolved.
preprocessing_override.adapter_trimming: Read cleaning follows the branch-specific preprocessing before minimap2 mapping.
preprocessing_override.length_quality_filtering: NanoFilt with Q >= 8 and minimum length 150 bp.
preprocessing_override.additional_preprocessing: Map to segment references, select the best reference with idxstats, then call consensus with BCFtools.

notes: If your entire project is dedicated to AIV chemistry benchmarking rather than wetland multi-omics, the Avian Influenza Profiling pipeline is usually the stronger exact fit.

title: Zambia eDNA Metabarcoding Pipeline

supported_entry_points.label: Nanopore FASTQ amplicon entry
supported_entry_points.notes: Single-end Nanopore amplicon reads are demultiplexed with OBITools.

supported_entry_points.label: Illumina paired-end amplicon entry
supported_entry_points.notes: Illumina reads are merged before shared downstream metabarcoding processing.

default_preprocessing.input_expectation: Basecalled Nanopore FASTQ or Illumina paired-end FASTQ for 12S / 16S metabarcoding.
default_preprocessing.basecalling: The published repo starts from basecalled reads; raw-signal basecalling is outside the documented workflow.
default_preprocessing.demultiplexing: Use obimultiplex with the provided demultiplexing reference tables.
default_preprocessing.adapter_trimming: Cutadapt primer trimming after read merging or demultiplexing.
default_preprocessing.length_quality_filtering: VSEARCH fastq filtering, followed by dereplication and chimera removal.
default_preprocessing.additional_preprocessing: OTU clustering at 99 percent and taxonomic assignment against MIDORI2.

execution_model: README-driven workflow with reproducibility tables and shared environment setup from the repository root.
notes: Best fit for water eDNA metabarcoding rather than shotgun metagenomics.
Explicitly documents both Nanopore single-end and Illumina paired-end amplicon entry points.
constraints: Does not document a raw POD5 or FAST5 basecalling branch.
Not a general shotgun metagenomics workflow.
decision_notes: Zambia eDNA is the better fit for general water eDNA metabarcoding than the wetland host-range branch.
This workflow is not suitable for shotgun or raw-signal-first questions.

title: Listeria Adaptive Sampling

supported_entry_points.label: Basecalled BAM to full Listeria workflow
supported_entry_points.notes: The published workflow usually starts from BAM and converts to FASTQ internally.

supported_entry_points.label: FASTQ entry for adapted runs
supported_entry_points.notes: FASTQ can be used for adapted runs when BAM is not the direct entry point.

default_preprocessing.input_expectation: Typically starts from basecalled BAM files, but can be adapted to FASTQ inputs.
default_preprocessing.basecalling: Raw-signal basecalling is not the main documented entry point; the published workflow usually starts from BAM produced upstream.
default_preprocessing.demultiplexing: Handle sample separation upstream if barcoded BAMs or FASTQs are still pooled.
default_preprocessing.adapter_trimming: Porechop after BAM to FASTQ conversion when trimming is still required.
default_preprocessing.length_quality_filtering: NanoFilt length filtering removes reads below 100 bp; the workflow also compiles yield and read-length summaries.
default_preprocessing.additional_preprocessing: Run Kraken2 before Listeria extraction, then compare AS versus native branches in the report.

execution_model: Script-based workflow oriented to SLURM, with a local wrapper and detailed supporting docs in the group repo plus a concise personal overview in Tim_Reska.
notes: Best fit for adaptive-sampling versus native-run comparison of Listeria recovery in complex food-safety samples.
Uses multiple assemblers and report generation tailored to target enrichment rather than generic metagenomics.
constraints: Dedicated to Listeria-focused recovery, not a general-purpose food microbiome workflow.
Exact published setup assumes matched adaptive-sampling and native comparisons.
decision_notes: This workflow is driven by target enrichment and AS-versus-native benchmarking rather than generic food microbiome analysis.
If your main goal is isolate assembly or clinical AMR rather than target recovery, use a different route.

title: AMR Nanopore

supported_entry_points.label: FAST5 isolate entry
supported_entry_points.notes: Basecall first, then proceed into isolate read cleaning and assembly.

supported_entry_points.label: FASTQ isolate entry
supported_entry_points.notes: Enter at trimming, filtering, and assembly.

default_preprocessing.input_expectation: FAST5 or FASTQ from clinical isolate sequencing.
default_preprocessing.basecalling: Use Guppy with dna_r9.4.1_450bps_hac when starting from FAST5.
default_preprocessing.demultiplexing: Not a central published requirement for this example workflow.
default_preprocessing.length_quality_filtering: NanoFilt with Q >= 9 and minimum length 200 bp.
default_preprocessing.additional_preprocessing: Proceed to Flye assembly, Minimap2 realignment, and Racon polishing.

execution_model: Interactive wrapper plus README-documented commands for a clinical isolate example dataset.
notes: Best fit for clinical isolate assembly and AMR profiling rather than metagenomic host-linkage analysis.
Documentation is example-driven around a Klebsiella pneumoniae KPC treatment study.
constraints: Not a clinical metagenomics workflow.
Not designed for raw POD5 methylation-aware processing.
decision_notes: This route is strongest for isolate-focused AMR profiling rather than multi-host association or plasmid network analysis.

title: CRE Plasmid Clustering

supported_entry_points.label: Raw POD5 to demuxed isolate assemblies
supported_entry_points.notes: Basecall and demultiplex with Dorado before isolate reconstruction.

supported_entry_points.label: FASTQ isolate entry
supported_entry_points.notes: Enter at cleaning and assembly if reads are already basecalled.

default_preprocessing.input_expectation: Raw POD5 or basecalled FASTQ from CRE isolate sequencing.
default_preprocessing.basecalling: Dorado SUP v5.0.0 for R10.4.1 RBK114.24 data.
default_preprocessing.demultiplexing: Dorado demux for barcode separation when pooled isolate data are still combined.
default_preprocessing.adapter_trimming: Porechop after demultiplexing; can be optional if newer Dorado trimming is already used upstream.
default_preprocessing.length_quality_filtering: NanoFilt with Q >= 8 and minimum length 200 bp.
default_preprocessing.additional_preprocessing: Run Flye, Medaka with --bacteria, then MOB-Suite, AMRFinderPlus, Mash, and Pling.

execution_model: Interactive wrapper plus detailed README with command-level documentation for plasmid reconstruction and clustering.
notes: Best fit for plasmid-focused relatedness analysis from CRE isolate data.
Explicitly documents RBK114.24 and barcode-based demultiplexing.
constraints: Not a general metagenomic pathogen-surveillance workflow.
Focused on isolate-scale plasmid reconstruction and clustering.
decision_notes: This route is for isolate-level plasmid comparison rather than generic AMR profiling or metagenomic host linkage.

title: Nanopore AMR Host Association

supported_entry_points.label: Raw POD5 to methylation-aware host association
supported_entry_points.notes: The workflow expects raw POD5 and performs basecalling, demultiplexing, and downstream synchronization internally.

default_preprocessing.input_expectation: Raw POD5 metagenomic data with barcode-aware sample handling.
default_preprocessing.basecalling: Dorado SUP branch from POD5 to basecalled BAM.
default_preprocessing.demultiplexing: Demultiplex basecalled BAMs before BAM to FASTQ conversion.
default_preprocessing.adapter_trimming: Use chopper / downstream filtering after BAM to FASTQ rather than the simpler isolate-style trim-only flow.
default_preprocessing.length_quality_filtering: Read filtering is performed before assembly so FASTQ and BAM stay synchronized for methylation-aware downstream analysis.
default_preprocessing.additional_preprocessing: Keep filtered BAM and FASTQ in sync, then assemble, polish, run Modkit, Nanomotif, MOB-Suite, AMRFinder, and Kraken2 annotation.

execution_model: Interactive wrapper plus standalone scripts run in a strict order from raw signal through methylation-based host association.
notes: Best fit for methylation-aware clinical metagenomics where AMR genes must be linked back to their hosts.
Kraken2 is used for annotation, while methylation signatures drive the host-association logic.
constraints: Requires raw POD5 entry and methylation-aware downstream analysis.
Not the right workflow for purified clinical isolates or generic plasmid clustering.
decision_notes: Kraken2 supports annotation here, but the actual host-association inference depends on methylation-aware analysis.
This is the closest clinical metagenome route in the collection.

title: Avian Influenza Profiling

supported_entry_points.label: Direct RNA basecalling entry
supported_entry_points.notes: Choose the chemistry-specific RNA002 or RNA004 basecalling script.

supported_entry_points.label: Basecalled FASTQ to consensus or assembly
supported_entry_points.notes: Enter at the de novo or reference-based influenza workflow.

default_preprocessing.input_expectation: Raw POD5 or already basecalled FASTQ for AIV-focused branches.
default_preprocessing.basecalling: Choose the branch that matches your chemistry: direct RNA002, direct RNA004, or cDNA plus RBK.
default_preprocessing.demultiplexing: Demultiplex only when barcoded cDNA or pooled inputs are still combined.
default_preprocessing.adapter_trimming: Follow the chemistry-specific branch before de novo or reference-based analysis.
default_preprocessing.length_quality_filtering: Branch-specific read cleaning precedes Flye, BCFTools consensus, or IRMA depending on the chosen approach.
default_preprocessing.additional_preprocessing: Use the exact basecalling script for your chemistry, then choose de novo, reference-based, or IRMA consensus assembly.

execution_model: Interactive wrapper that routes to chemistry-specific scripts for direct RNA, cDNA, and influenza-focused consensus workflows.
notes: Best fit for chemistry benchmarking and AIV-specific consensus generation, subtyping, and phylogeny.
Supports direct RNA and cDNA branches rather than only one influenza chemistry.
constraints: Targeted to avian influenza rather than untargeted viral-metagenome discovery.
Choose the exact chemistry branch before running downstream scripts.
decision_notes: This is the strongest direct RNA or cDNA AIV route in the collection outside the wetland umbrella workflow.

title: From Feather to Fur

supported_entry_points.label: FASTQ entry for variant calling
supported_entry_points.notes: Run the variant-calling stage from reads and reference.

supported_entry_points.label: VCF entry for downstream comparison
supported_entry_points.notes: Enter at comparative filtering and consensus generation.

default_preprocessing.input_expectation: Basecalled FASTQ and/or VCF files for avian-to-mammalian comparison workflows.
default_preprocessing.basecalling: Basecalling is upstream of the documented variant-comparison workflow.
default_preprocessing.demultiplexing: Usually not a central step in the documented downstream analysis.
default_preprocessing.adapter_trimming: Perform any upstream read cleaning before variant calling with minimap2 and Clair3.
default_preprocessing.length_quality_filtering: Apply read filtering before mapping if starting from FASTQ rather than VCF.
default_preprocessing.additional_preprocessing: Generate filtered VCFs, consensus FASTA, FluSurver annotations, and merged variant interpretation tables.

execution_model: Interactive wrapper plus downstream variant-processing steps for comparative host-transition analysis.
notes: Best fit for downstream variant interpretation across matched avian and mammalian host samples.
The published README assumes at least some upstream variant-calling or consensus-generation work is already understood.
constraints: Not a general influenza-consensus pipeline.
FluSurver interpretation remains a manual downstream component in the documented workflow.
decision_notes: This workflow is specifically for cross-host comparison after or alongside variant calling rather than first-pass AIV consensus generation.

title: Squiggle4Viability

supported_entry_points.label: Raw signal AI inference
supported_entry_points.notes: Enter directly into segmentation, preprocessing, and model inference.

default_preprocessing.input_expectation: Raw FAST5 or POD5 files used directly for segmentation, preprocessing, model inference, or signal explainability.
default_preprocessing.basecalling: Standard basecalling is not the primary entry point for the core viability model. Raw signal remains the central input.
default_preprocessing.demultiplexing: Not part of the main AI viability branch, though the repository also contains a secondary metagenomics section.
default_preprocessing.adapter_trimming: Not part of the main signal-level model entry point.
default_preprocessing.length_quality_filtering: Signal segmentation and tensor preprocessing replace the standard read-level trimming and filtering logic.
default_preprocessing.additional_preprocessing: Requires the dedicated GPU-capable requirements set for model training or inference.

execution_model: Interactive wrapper with a branch between AI inference on raw signal and the broader metagenomics-oriented utilities in the repo.
notes: Best fit when the biological question is viability directly from nanopore signal rather than standard downstream assembly or taxonomic profiling.
Includes AI training, inference, explainability, and a secondary metagenomics section.
constraints: Requires distinct dependencies and usually a GPU-capable environment.
Not a substitute for conventional basecalled-read pipelines when the main objective is standard metagenomic analysis.
decision_notes: This is the only raw-signal-first viability workflow in the collection and should not be treated as a normal taxonomic pipeline.
